what_it_does: CSS stylesheet that styles the feedback subsystem (right-click affordance, context menu, capture modal, chat, toast, learnings badge) and Vi's Memory panel (launch button, overlay, panel, tabs, search, filters, memory cards, feedback cards, empty state, footer).
observations: Uses BEM-style class naming throughout, e.g. .fb-menu__item and .mem-card__text.
Relies heavily on CSS custom properties such as --accent-gold, --ink, --fg-secondary, and radius variables.
Feedback context menu is fixed positioned at z-index 200 with a scale/fade pop-in animation.
Modal overlay uses backdrop-filter blur and a warm gradient background shared by the context menu.
Chat UI supports user/assistant bubbles, a typing-dots indicator, and a pill-shaped input with send button.
Memory panel is a large fixed overlay with tabs, search, filter chips, cards, inline editing, toggle switches, and meta actions.
Memory cards have kind badges (.mem-kind--avoid, .mem-kind--should_ask, .mem-kind--should_show), scope tags, and a fired indicator.
Includes a learnings badge (.fb-learn-badge) and a memory launch button with a count pill.
Responsive sizing uses min() and calc() for modal widths and heights.
Some animations (voice-fade, intake-blink) and many custom properties are referenced but not defined in this file.
standouts: The memory panel UI is unusually elaborate for a stylesheet, covering tabs, search, filters, editable cards, toggles, and meta links.
A consistent warm gold/cream visual theme unifies both lightweight feedback widgets and the full memory panel.
The file mixes ephemeral components (toast, context menu) with a persistent curation interface in one sheet.
conventions: CSS custom properties for colors, typography, radii, and easing.
Flexbox for layout and grid place-items for centering.
Modifier classes like .on and .mem-card--off for state.
Mobile-first responsive dimensions via min() and calc().
concerns: Keyframes voice-fade and intake-blink are referenced but not defined in this file.
Many CSS variables are used without being declared here, creating a dependency on external/global styles.
No focus-visible or reduced-motion styles are present.
No explicit z-index scale documentation within the file.
notes: A warm-themed, BEM-organized stylesheet that implements both lightweight feedback affordances and a full memory curation panel for Vi.
questions: Are the undefined keyframe animations voice-fade and intake-blink provided by a global stylesheet?
Is the z-index layering (190, 195, 200, 210) coordinated with other overlays elsewhere in the app?
What triggers the .mem-fired state and how is it communicated to users?
purpose_vs_actual: The header states the file covers the feedback subsystem and Vi's Memory panel, which matches the selectors and sections present.
apparent_themes: Modular component architecture via BEM naming.
intention_signals: To give users a polished, cohesive interface for reporting feedback, chatting about issues, and inspecting or editing the AI assistant Vi's learned memories, all within a consistent warm visual system.
novelty: It combines ephemeral feedback widgets and a full memory-management panel in a single stylesheet, using a distinctive gold-on-cream palette and detailed card-level controls.
contribution: A self-contained visual specification for feedback and memory UI components, including context menus, modals, chat bubbles, toasts, filter chips, memory cards, toggles, and inline editing patterns.
summary_for_embedding: CSS stylesheet defining warm-themed component styles for a feedback subsystem and Vi's Memory panel, including context menus, modals, chat, toasts, memory cards, toggles, and filters.
intention_for_embedding: To style the user-facing feedback capture flow and the memory curation interface for an AI assistant named Vi.
